input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Why |
|---|---|---|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | Compilers & CLI utilities |
| **.NET SDK (latest LTS or STS)** | [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) | .NET runtime + CLI |
| **.NET MAUI workload** | Installed via `dotnet workload install maui` | MAUI project templates & build targets |
| **JDK 17** | Bundled with the MAUI workload (Microsoft OpenJDK) — or manually from [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download) | Android build toolchain |
| **Android SDK** | Auto-provisioned by MAUI workload, or install via Android Studio from [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) | Android emulator, platform APIs |

## 2. Installation Steps (in order)

```bash
# 1 — Install Xcode from the App Store, then accept the license:
sudo xcodebuild -license accept
xcode-select --install

# 2 — Install .NET SDK (grab the latest .NET 8 or 9 LTS installer from dot.net):
# Run the downloaded .pkg, then verify:
dotnet --version

# 3 — Install the MAUI workload (handles Android SDK + JDK automatically):
sudo dotnet workload install maui

# 4 — (Optional) Install Android Studio if you want the full emulator manager UI
```

## 3. Discover the Right Versions

```bash
# See available .NET SDK versions:
dotnet --list-sdks

# See installed workloads and check for updates:
dotnet workload list
dotnet workload update

# See which Android/iOS SDKs the MAUI workload pulled in:
dotnet workload search maui
```

Check the official support matrix at:
**[learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms)**

## 4. Verify Everything Works

```bash
# Create and build a test app:
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode):
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios        # or net9.0-ios

# Build for Android:
dotnet build -f net8.0-android    # or net9.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator:
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios -t:Run

# Run on Android Emulator (start one first via Android Studio or AVD manager):
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Run
```

## 5. Key Gotchas on macOS

- **Apple Silicon**: .NET and MAUI support arm64 natively — use the Arm64 installer.
- **Xcode version**: MAUI releases are pinned to specific Xcode versions. Check the [MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) for compatibility.
- **Android emulator on Apple Silicon**: Use an arm64-v8a system image (x86_64 images won't work without Rosetta/translation).
- **`sudo` for workloads**: On macOS the .NET install path is often root-owned, so `sudo dotnet workload install` is typically needed.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI development on macOS targeting iOS and Android. They can't run commands — they just want guidance. This is a knowledge question, not a coding task. I should provide a concise, authoritative answer directly.
